raw_input:
[5.0/5] Buy it! I am 66 years old and this is the BEST razor I've ever used! It shaves close, and very fast. I can't recommend it highly enough. It comes with a bikini attachment, but it shaves wonderfully without it! Don't hesitate to buy it. [5.0/5] My first electric shaver - a win! I received this product as a gift in Nov 09 and have been very pleased with it. I have never had an electric shaver before, and thought it might be a time/energy/money-saver. So far, so good! It seems to work better dry, rather than wet, which is actually better for me (shower is too small to shave in anyway!). I learned to go slow, otherwise it needs a second pass, but I don't expect other electric razors are much different. The trimmer works pretty well, although I'm still getting the hang of it. The charger is small and tends to be unobstrusive; the cord is long, which is nice.
Overall, I've been very pleased to not be using a razor and cream!! [2.0/5] WIfe disapponted - I know Panasonic can make better products than this! Looking forward to Panasonic creating the same quality razor for women as they do for men.

This 'top of the line' electric razor was encased in plastic and hard to open. The materials used in the razor itself unfortunately matched that lack of quality. Cutting heads were rudimentary and did not provide a close or even shave. My wife is going back to a blade not because she loves it, but at least it works as an effective tool. [2.0/5] I want my Remington back I had a Remington wet/dry that finally got outdated and I thought to replace it. I did a lot of research and decided to try this Panasonic. I was really excited when it got here and the first time I used it I loved it and was all set to write a raving review. But I thought I should wait a couple weeks. I am very unhappy with it, as it turns out. I wish I had my Remington back (or got a new Remington). Yes, the Remington is a little louder. But this one is loud too - you can hear both of them from many rooms away. So really, that's not a big deal. I ended up getting another Remington and it is much better.

What got me is two things with this one:
1) The sides that hold the razor in place are taller than the razor area. So when you go to get a close shave, the sides of the unit scratch/scrape your leg. It is plastic, so no blood drawn or anything, but it doesn't feel good. Has no one else noticed this? I went to do a quick shave on my calves and ended up with stripes from the sides of the unit scraping my legs. And it didn't even get the little stubble. I didn't think the Remington was that fantastic, but it did better than this. Someone suggested to put the side extenders down. I do see the extenders (per the angry reply to this post), but they are down all the way. The won't go any farther down. So your angry post is not applicable. If I did not apply force to my leg with the razor, the length of the stubble it left was ridiculous. Again, maybe very long stubble is just the way it is, but not with my two Remingtons. They got much closer without having to apply force.

2) It vents out the cut hair once in a while all over your leg. You could be shaving along la di da and all of a sudden get a huge area of hair. I thought "I already went over that area?" but it was just the hair that got spit out from the razor. It is a little gross, I'm always wiping the cut hair off my leg. I've had two Remington's that did not do this. The Remington might have left the hair on the ground, or kept it in its head for me to clean later, but I prefer the Remington that didn't spit out puffs of hair on my legs incrementally. Again, the angry reply to this about including a vacuum is a bit silly.

Another disadvantage is that this one's charger plugs straight into the outlet, instead of having a cord. So I have to find an outlet to leave it in to charge that my kids won't get to. I like the cord better, so I can plug the cord in behind my dresser and have it sitting on top of my dresser in the little charger base. This one, you have to put the razor in the charger directly into the outlet. I could deal with this though if it didn't spit hair out all over my leg or leave scrapes down my leg. Yes, you could get an extension cord, so it's not that big of a deal. Just one more thing to add to a poor design IMO. [5.0/5] Great razor I've owned several electric razors in the past and this one is by far superior to the others. It gives you a close shave without causing any nicks or burns like my old one. It can be used either wet or dry and works well both ways. I also like the fact that you can change the angle of the head to shave your armpits vs your legs. Of course it will not compare to a regular razor but for a quick convenient shave, it works great. You will need to use it every day or two if your hair grows quickly. Well worth the money in my opinion. [2.0/5] Not a good basic shaver At 62, my shaving needs are basic, so I have nothing to say about the bikini features, for obvious reasons. I don't particularly care to shave in the shower either. All I want in a shaver is for it to work well for my armpits and legs. I have sensitive skin, so I took a chance on this shaver; it's my first Panasonic, and my last.

The reason this gets two stars instead of one is that it does do a good job on my legs, and the shaver holds a charge well, for several shaves. That said, my primary concern is shaving my armpits without irritation, which it does, and without having to keep going over the same area to catch all the hairs, which it doesn't. You would think that with the rotating head that this contour feature would help, but this shaver will not give you a close shave under your arms without several passes. I don't even have thick hair, so if you do, I certainly wouldn't recommend this shaver. [3.0/5] Good for occasional use... This is a pretty shaver; a nice lavender/purple color. I like that it has the base that you can leave it on while unplugged, so storage is not an issue. It shaved pretty close the first time, although, I would have to use it everyday to keep my legs looking halfway decent, and that is too time consuming. It does not shave anything like a manual razor, nowhere near as close, so stubble is a constant. But I do suffer from dizziness problems, so it will be good to use when I am too dizzy to risk getting into the tub/shower, or for quick shaves when going somewhere. I will have to charge it before every shave, as the charge ran out halfway through my second shave, so I have to think of this ahead of time, no big deal. It is a nice electric razor for what it does. It did not scratch or cut my skin, even around my knobby knees. Just keep in mind that electric razors do not shave anything like a manual shaver; no where near as close. If you grow hair quickly like I do, this is an issue. [5.0/5] Really happy with this shaver It's worth paying a little extra for this one. It's much much better than the last shaver that I had. [4.0/5] Would never go back to regular razors... I've had my eye on this for awhile, and finally got it for Christmas. There were a few key reasons I wanted one. First, wet/dry shaving sounded great, especially dry without wasting water down the drain. Also, the cost of replacement razors for the venus and similar items is ridiculous, so I'd only change it when it got very dull. Plus, I hated throwing away the plastic and adding to the landfills. The reviews of this seemed promising, and I'm glad to say, I definitely agree!

The razor works really well dry shaving, and its so much easier then balancing precariously on one foot in a wet shower. Not too loud, once you get started you forget it's making any noise. According to the package, you need to replace the foils once a year and the razors in the machine every two years. Imagine the money I'll save! Especially if you go thru multiple 8 packs of venus razors a year. It doesn't get as close a shave as the regular razor, but thats not a big deal for me. I'll use this on a normal basis and get out the razor when I need the babysoft feel.

I'm a big fan, and will never go back to using traditional razors full time. [5.0/5] Fabulous! I was tired of nicks and the hassle and waste of water (I live in the desert) of shaving with a blade. I was wary of buying an electric shaver because I have purchased both shavers and epilators in the past and found they did not work and/or were just too painful to use. Having read the reviews for this one on Amazon, I decided to take a chance since the price was right. All I can say is, WOW! I am really impressed by this shaver! It is not quite as close as a blade shave, but really good. I saved a lot of time, and a lot of water (even better!). It also did not irritate my underarm area like some electric shavers I have used. I am very pleased with this purchase and would highly recommend it to others. [1.0/5] Save Your Money I was not crazy about this product, 3 months later it hardly works at all. I have to go over the same patch of skin before it cuts all the hairs which leads to skin irritation also it's not a close shave so while it worked I found myself having to shave every morning. [5.0/5] Great Little Shaver This shaver works extremely well. I get a very close shave and it leaves my skin silky smooth. It is easy to clean. I have very sensitive skin and have had no outbreaks or razer burn from this little shaver. [5.0/5] Excellent Woman's Electric Shaver This is the best electric shaver I have ever owned. Besides the fact, it is one of the best electric razors. The price is more than fair beause I have seen the same exact item for more money than what Amazon charged for it.

I would definitely recommend this item. [5.0/5] great for legs! I love this shaver. It charges quickly and stays charged. It removes leg hair painlessly without leaving stubbles. The grip is easy to use and it is easy to clean. I travel with it as well. I didn't find it that helpful for removing underarm hair but it is fantastic for the entire leg, leaving a soft smooth feel to the touch. [4.0/5] Great choice for girl's first shaver It was time for my granddaughter to begin shaving. But razors can be a bit scary with sharp blades! Even though an electric shaver does not shave as close as a blade shaver, it also does not nick! This is a pretty device (I ordered the mauve, kind of a pearlized pink color) and is easy to hold and to use. It holds a charge for a long time. For closest results, go over the area in every direction and do both straight and circular movements. [5.0/5] Highly Recommend! I have never written a product review online before but my experience with this razor definitely deserves one! I recieved this on Wed, charged it and began use on Thursday. I have used it once wet and one dry and loved the results of both! It is quiet, provides a close shave already (claims to improve over three weeks!), and gives me NO razor burn. The whole reason I bought this razor was to try to find something that would eliminate razor burn for me and I had yet to succeed after trying all kinds of epilators, razors, depilatory creams, and other electric razors. This finally has me happy! It may not give the perfect smooth shave that my sister always seems to get right out of the box but I'm very satsified! I was never able to shave more than twice a week before so this will give me a closer shave with a better experience hands down! Can't wait to see what happens after the 3 week point! [4.0/5] works as promised It was nice that it came already charged. I have used it several times and have not needed to charge it. I like that the charger is small. It sits in the outlet and the shaver sits on top of it. I have a small bathroom and I don't want another bulky base and extra attachments laying around. I use it when I don't have time to shave. You can still feel the hair but you can't see it.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Most customers are very happy with this electric razor, giving it high ratings. Many praise it for providing a close, comfortable shave without nicks or razor burn, and appreciate that it works both wet and dry. Customers like that it is easy to use, easy to clean, and comes with useful attachments like a bikini trimmer. Several reviewers mention it saves time, water, and money compared to disposable razors. The compact charger design is generally seen as a plus, though a few wish it had a cord instead of plugging directly into the wall. It is also noted as a good option for those new to shaving or looking for a safer alternative to blade razors.

On the negative side, a handful of customers were disappointed. Some felt it did not shave as closely as a traditional blade razor and required multiple passes. One reviewer had issues with the sides of the unit scraping their legs, and another was bothered by cut hairs being expelled onto the skin during shaving. One customer reported the razor stopped working well after just three months. A few noted it caused irritation, particularly in sensitive areas like the armpits. Overall, the razor seems well suited for casual or occasional use but may not fully satisfy those expecting results equal to a manual razor.
